html:
𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄

THE ANCIENT COVENANT RECORD

Volume Eighteen — Part Six

The Book of Yirmeyahu

YIRMEYAHU

The Book of Jeremiah — DSS Shorter Recension Primary

Chapters 40 through 48

The Remnant in Egypt — The Word to Barukh — Oracles Against Egypt — The Pelishtim — Moav

PRIMARY MANUSCRIPT AUTHORITIES

4QJer-a and 4QJer-b — Dead Sea Scrolls — Cave 4 Qumran

Ethiopian Orit Ge'ez — Complete Canon

YHWH and The Creator only

COMPARATIVE NOTES AT END OF EVERY CHAPTER

DSS · ORIT GE'EZ · MASORETIC VARIANT · CRITICAL NOTE

YHWH / 𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄 and The Creator only — no substitute titles

All personal names in original Hebrew form throughout

YIRMEYAHU — CHAPTERS 40 THROUGH 48 — THE REMNANT AND THE ORACLES AGAINST THE NATIONS

YIRMEYAHU — CHAPTER 40 Yirmeyahu Released by Nevuzaradan — Gedalyahu Appointed Governor — The Remnant Gathers at Mitzpah

1 The word that came to Yirmeyahu from YHWH after Nevuzaradan the captain of the guard had let him go from Ramah, when he took him bound in chains along with all the captives of Yerushalayim and Yehudah who were being exiled to Babylon.

2 The captain of the guard took Yirmeyahu and said to him, YHWH your Creator pronounced this disaster against this place.

3 YHWH has brought it about, and has done as he said. Because you sinned against YHWH and did not obey his voice, this thing has come upon you.

4 Now behold, I release you today from the chains on your hands. If it seems good to you to come with me to Babylon, come, and I will look after you well. But if it seems wrong to you to come with me to Babylon, remain here. See, the whole land is before you — go wherever you think it good and right to go.

5 If you remain, then return to Gedalyahu the son of Akhikam, son of Shafan, whom the king of Babylon appointed governor of the cities of Yehudah, and dwell with him among the people. Or go wherever you think it right to go. So the captain of the guard gave him an allowance of food and a present, and let him go.

6 Then Yirmeyahu went to Gedalyahu the son of Akhikam, at Mitzpah, and lived with him among the people who were left in the land.

7 When all the captains of the forces in the open country and their men heard that the king of Babylon had appointed Gedalyahu the son of Akhikam governor in the land and had committed to him men, women, and children, those of the poorest of the land who had not been taken into exile to Babylon,

8 They went to Gedalyahu at Mitzpah — Yishma'el the son of Netanyahu, Yokhanan and Yonatan the sons of Kare'akh, Serayahu the son of Tanhumeth, the sons of Efai the Netofathite, and Yezanyahu the son of the Ma'akhathite, they and their men.

9 Gedalyahu the son of Akhikam, son of Shafan, swore to them and their men, saying, Do not be afraid to serve the Chaldeans. Dwell in the land and serve the king of Babylon, and it shall be well with you.

10 As for me, I will dwell at Mitzpah, to stand before the Chaldeans who will come to us. But as for you, gather wine and summer fruits and oil, and store them in your vessels, and dwell in your cities that you have taken.

11 Likewise, when all the Yehudim who were in Moav and among the Ammonites and in Edom and in other lands heard that the king of Babylon had left a remnant in Yehudah and had appointed over them Gedalyahu the son of Akhikam, son of Shafan,

12 Then all the Yehudim returned from all the places to which they had been driven and came to the land of Yehudah, to Gedalyahu at Mitzpah. And they gathered wine and summer fruits in great abundance.

13 Now Yokhanan the son of Kare'akh and all the leaders of the forces in the open country came to Gedalyahu at Mitzpah

14 And said to him, Do you know that Ba'alis the king of the Ammonites has sent Yishma'el the son of Netanyahu to take your life? But Gedalyahu the son of Akhikam would not believe them.

15 Then Yokhanan the son of Kare'akh spoke secretly to Gedalyahu at Mitzpah, saying, Please let me go and strike down Yishma'el the son of Netanyahu — no one will know it. Why should he take your life, so that all the Yehudim who are gathered around you would be scattered, and the remnant of Yehudah would perish?

16 But Gedalyahu the son of Akhikam said to Yokhanan the son of Kare'akh, You shall not do this thing, for you are speaking falsely of Yishma'el.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The DSS shorter recension preserves Chapter 40 in close agreement with the Masoretic. The aftermath of the fall — the covenant remnant gathering at Mitzpah under Gedalyahu — is the covenant attempt at covenant continuation in the land. Nevuzaradan, the Babylonian captain, accurately summarizes the covenant reason for the disaster — verse 2-3 — demonstrating that the covenant truth is evident even to foreign military commanders.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 40 in full. Verse 4 — the whole land is before you — go wherever you think it good and right to go — is the covenant freedom extended to Yirmeyahu by the Babylonian commander: the covenant prophet who was imprisoned by his own king is released and given free movement by the conqueror. The covenant irony is complete.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 40. Verse 9 — do not be afraid to serve the Chaldeans — dwell in the land and serve the king of Babylon, and it shall be well with you — is Gedalyahu's covenant echo of Yirmeyahu's own word throughout the book. Covenant submission to Babylon produces welfare — resistance produces destruction.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 14-16 — the warning about Yishma'el's plot against Gedalyahu. Yokhanan warns Gedalyahu of the assassination plan — Gedalyahu refuses to believe him. This is the covenant tragedy of misplaced covenant trust: Gedalyahu extends covenant good faith to a man planning his murder. The covenant community's last hope for covenant continuation in the land dies not by Babylonian sword but by a Yehudite assassin with Ammonite backing.

YIRMEYAHU — CHAPTER 41 Yishma'el Murders Gedalyahu — Eighty Men Slain — Yokhanan Rescues the Captives — They Prepare to Go to Egypt

1 In the seventh month, Yishma'el the son of Netanyahu, son of Elishama, of the royal family, one of the chief officers of the king, came with ten men to Gedalyahu the son of Akhikam, at Mitzpah. As they ate bread together there at Mitzpah,

2 Yishma'el the son of Netanyahu and the ten men with him rose up and struck down Gedalyahu the son of Akhikam, son of Shafan, with the sword, and killed him, whom the king of Babylon had appointed governor in the land.

3 Yishma'el also struck down all the Yehudim who were with Gedalyahu at Mitzpah, and the Chaldean soldiers who happened to be there.

4 On the day after the murder of Gedalyahu, before anyone knew of it,

5 Eighty men arrived from Shekhem and Shiloh and Samaria, with their beards shaved and their clothes torn, and their bodies gashed, bringing grain offerings and incense to present at the house of YHWH.

6 And Yishma'el the son of Netanyahu came out from Mitzpah to meet them, weeping as he came. As he met them, he said to them, Come in to Gedalyahu the son of Akhikam.

7 When they came into the city, Yishma'el the son of Netanyahu and the men with him slaughtered them and cast them into a cistern.

8 But there were ten men among them who said to Yishma'el, Do not put us to death, for we have stores of wheat, barley, oil, and honey hidden in the fields. So he refrained and did not put them to death with their companions.

9 Now the cistern into which Yishma'el had thrown all the bodies of the men whom he had struck down because of Gedalyahu was the large cistern that King Asa had made for defense against Baasha king of Yisra'EL. Yishma'el the son of Netanyahu filled it with the slain.

10 Then Yishma'el took captive all the rest of the people who were in Mitzpah, the king's daughters and all the people who were left at Mitzpah, whom Nevuzaradan the captain of the guard had committed to Gedalyahu the son of Akhikam. Yishma'el the son of Netanyahu took them captive and set out to cross over to the Ammonites.

11 But when Yokhanan the son of Kare'akh and all the leaders of the forces with him heard of all the evil that Yishma'el the son of Netanyahu had done,

12 They took all their men and went to fight against Yishma'el the son of Netanyahu. They came upon him at the great pool that is in Givon.

13 And when all the people who were with Yishma'el saw Yokhanan the son of Kare'akh and all the leaders of the forces with him, they were glad.

14 So all the people whom Yishma'el had taken captive from Mitzpah turned around and came back, and went to Yokhanan the son of Kare'akh.

15 But Yishma'el the son of Netanyahu escaped from Yokhanan with eight men, and went to the Ammonites.

16 Then Yokhanan the son of Kare'akh and all the leaders of the forces with him took all the remnant of the people whom Yishma'el the son of Netanyahu had taken captive from Mitzpah, after he had struck down Gedalyahu the son of Akhikam — soldiers, women, children, and eunuchs, whom Yokhanan brought back from Givon.

17 And they went and stayed at Gerut Kimham near Beit-Lekhem, intending to go to Egypt

18 Because of the Chaldeans. For they were afraid of them, because Yishma'el the son of Netanyahu had struck down Gedalyahu the son of Akhikam, whom the king of Babylon had appointed governor over the land.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The DSS shorter recension preserves Chapter 41 in close agreement with the Masoretic. The assassination of Gedalyahu ends the covenant experiment of the post-fall remnant community in the land. The covenant remnant YHWH had preserved — including the poor given vineyards and fields in Chapter 39:10 — is shattered by internal covenant violence. The covenant tragedy after the covenant tragedy.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 41 in full. The eighty men of verse 5 — coming from Shekhem, Shiloh, and Samaria with offerings for the house of YHWH — are covenant pilgrims who do not know the governor they are coming to honor is already dead. Their slaughter in the cistern is the covenant nadir of the post-fall period.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 41. Verse 17-18 — they intended to go to Egypt because of the Chaldeans, for they were afraid. The covenant fear that will drive the remnant to Egypt — the very place YHWH has warned them not to return to — is the final covenant choice of the post-fall generation. Fear of Babylon drives them toward Egypt.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 2 — Yishma'el struck down Gedalyahu, whom the king of Babylon had appointed governor. The covenant detail: Gedalyahu's appointment by Babylon is the legitimate governing authority for the post-fall remnant — the covenant arrangement YHWH authorized. Yishma'el's assassination is not merely murder but the destruction of the covenant structure YHWH put in place. The covenant remnant's last ordered existence in the land ends here.

YIRMEYAHU — CHAPTER 42 The Remnant Asks Yirmeyahu — If You Remain in This Land I Will Build You — Do Not Go to Egypt — Fear Not the King of Babylon

1 Then all the commanders of the forces, and Yokhanan the son of Kare'akh and Yezanyahu the son of Hoshayahu, and all the people from the least to the greatest, came near

2 And said to Yirmeyahu the prophet, Let our plea for mercy come before you, and pray to YHWH your Creator for us, for all this remnant — for we are left with but a few, as your eyes see us —

3 That YHWH your Creator may tell us the way we should go, and the thing that we should do.

4 Yirmeyahu the prophet said to them, I have heard you. Behold, I will pray to YHWH your Creator according to your request, and whatever YHWH answers you I will tell you. I will keep nothing back from you.

5 Then they said to Yirmeyahu, May YHWH be a true and faithful witness against us if we do not act according to all the word with which YHWH your Creator sends you to us.

6 Whether it is good or evil, we will obey the voice of YHWH our Creator to whom we are sending you, that it may be well with us when we obey the voice of YHWH our Creator.

7 At the end of ten days the word of YHWH came to Yirmeyahu.

8 Then he summoned Yokhanan the son of Kare'akh and all the commanders of the forces who were with him, and all the people from the least to the greatest.

9 And he said to them, Thus says YHWH, the Creator of Yisra'EL, to whom you sent me to present your plea for mercy before him:

10 If you will remain in this land, then I will build you up and not pull you down, and I will plant you and not pluck you up. For I relent of the disaster that I did to you.

11 Do not fear the king of Babylon, of whom you are afraid. Do not fear him, declares YHWH, for I am with you, to save you and to deliver you from his hand.

12 I will grant you mercy, that he may have mercy on you and let you remain in your own land.

13 But if you say, We will not remain in this land, disobeying the voice of YHWH your Creator

14 And saying, No, we will go to the land of Egypt, where we shall not see war or hear the sound of the trumpet or be hungry for bread, and we will dwell there,

15 Then hear the word of YHWH, O remnant of Yehudah. Thus says YHWH of hosts, the Creator of Yisra'EL: If you set your faces to enter Egypt and go to live there,

16 Then the sword that you fear shall overtake you there in the land of Egypt, and the famine of which you are afraid shall follow close after you to Egypt, and there you shall die.

17 All the men who set their faces to go to Egypt to live there shall die by the sword, by famine, and by pestilence. They shall have no remnant or survivor from the disaster that I will bring upon them.

18 For thus says YHWH of hosts, the Creator of Yisra'EL: As my anger and my wrath were poured out on the inhabitants of Yerushalayim, so my wrath will be poured out on you when you go to Egypt. You shall become an execration, a horror, a curse, and a taunt. You shall see this place no more.

19 YHWH has said to you, O remnant of Yehudah, Do not go to Egypt. Know for a certainty that I have warned you this day

20 That you have gone astray at the cost of your lives. For you sent me to YHWH your Creator, saying, Pray for us to YHWH our Creator, and whatever YHWH our Creator says, tell us and we will do it.

21 And I have this day told you, but you have not obeyed the voice of YHWH your Creator in anything that he sent me to tell you.

22 Now therefore know for a certainty that you shall die by the sword, by famine, and by pestilence in the place where you desire to go to live.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The DSS shorter recension preserves Chapter 42 in close agreement with the Masoretic. The covenant consultation with Yirmeyahu — the remnant swearing to obey whatever YHWH says — followed immediately by their rejection of the covenant word — is the covenant pattern of the entire book compressed into a single episode: covenant oath, covenant word, covenant rejection.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 42 in full. Verse 10 — if you will remain in this land, then I will build you up and not pull you down, and I will plant you and not pluck you up — is the covenant restoration promise contingent on covenant obedience to remain in the land. The same vocabulary of plucking up and building down from Chapter 1:10 is now offered in reverse.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 42. Verse 5-6 — the remnant's covenant oath: may YHWH be a true and faithful witness against us if we do not act according to all the word — whether good or evil, we will obey. The covenant oath is comprehensive and binding. Its immediate violation in Chapters 43-44 is the final covenant breach of the book's narrative.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 20-21 — you have gone astray at the cost of your lives — you sent me to YHWH saying we will do whatever he says — and I have told you, but you have not obeyed. Yirmeyahu names the covenant breach precisely: they made a covenant oath to obey the covenant word before hearing it, then rejected it when it contradicted their pre-made decision. The consultation was not genuine covenant inquiry — it was covenant validation-seeking for a decision already made.

YIRMEYAHU — CHAPTER 43 They Go to Egypt — Barukh Has Incited You — Yirmeyahu in Takhpanes — The Stones Hidden at Pharaoh's Palace

1 When Yirmeyahu finished speaking to all the people all these words of YHWH their Creator, with which YHWH their Creator had sent him to them,

2 Azaryahu the son of Hoshayahu and Yokhanan the son of Kare'akh and all the insolent men said to Yirmeyahu, You are telling a lie. YHWH our Creator did not send you to say, Do not go to Egypt to live there,

3 But Barukh the son of Neriyahu has set you against us, to deliver us into the hand of the Chaldeans, that they may kill us or take us into exile to Babylon.

4 So Yokhanan the son of Kare'akh and all the commanders of the forces and all the people did not obey the voice of YHWH, to remain in the land of Yehudah.

5 But Yokhanan the son of Kare'akh and all the commanders of the forces took all the remnant of Yehudah who had returned to live in the land of Yehudah from all the nations to which they had been driven —

6 Men, women, children, the king's daughters, and every person whom Nevuzaradan the captain of the guard had left with Gedalyahu the son of Akhikam, son of Shafan, and Yirmeyahu the prophet and Barukh the son of Neriyahu —

7 And they came into the land of Egypt, for they did not obey the voice of YHWH. And they arrived at Takhpanes.

8 Then the word of YHWH came to Yirmeyahu in Takhpanes:

9 Take in your hands large stones and hide them in the mortar in the pavement that is at the entrance to Pharaoh's palace in Takhpanes, in the sight of the men of Yehudah,

10 And say to them, Thus says YHWH of hosts, the Creator of Yisra'EL: Behold, I will send and take Nevukhadnetzar the king of Babylon, my servant, and I will set his throne above these stones that I have hidden, and he will spread his royal canopy over them.

11 He shall come and strike the land of Egypt, giving over to pestilence those who are doomed to pestilence, to captivity those who are doomed to captivity, and to the sword those who are doomed to the sword.

12 He shall kindle a fire in the temples of the gods of Egypt, and he shall burn them and carry them away captive. And he shall clean the land of Egypt as a shepherd cleans his cloak of vermin, and he shall go away from there in peace.

13 He shall break the obelisks of Heliopolis, which is in the land of Egypt, and the temples of the gods of Egypt he shall burn with fire.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The DSS shorter recension preserves Chapter 43 in close agreement with the Masoretic. The descent to Egypt — the covenant reversal of the Exodus — is the final covenant act of the book's historical narrative. The remnant goes the direction of Egypt: back into the covenant house of slavery from which YHWH had delivered their fathers. The covenant stones hidden in Takhpanes declare that Babylon will follow them there.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 43 in full. Verse 10 — I will send and take Nevukhadnetzar the king of Babylon, my servant, and I will set his throne above these stones. The covenant stones Yirmeyahu hides at Pharaoh's palace become the foundation stones of Nevukhadnetzar's throne. The covenant word follows the covenant people into Egypt.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 43. Verse 2-3 — Azaryahu accuses Barukh of inciting Yirmeyahu against them. The covenant accusation against the prophet's scribe is the covenant community's final attempt to discredit the covenant word by attacking its human instrument. When the covenant word is unwelcome, the messenger is blamed.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 7 — and they came into the land of Egypt, for they did not obey the voice of YHWH. The covenant summary of the entire remnant narrative after the fall: they asked for the covenant word, received the covenant word, swore to obey the covenant word, and disobeyed the covenant word. The Exodus — the foundational covenant event of Yisra'EL's identity — is reversed. The people return to the land of their slavery by their own covenant choice.

YIRMEYAHU — CHAPTER 44 The Word to the Yehudim in Egypt — The Queen of Heaven — We Will Not Listen to You — A Small Number Shall Return

1 The word that came to Yirmeyahu concerning all the Yehudim who lived in the land of Egypt, at Migdol, at Takhpanes, at Nof, and in the land of Patros:

2 Thus says YHWH of hosts, the Creator of Yisra'EL: You have seen all the disaster that I brought upon Yerushalayim and upon all the cities of Yehudah. Behold, this day they are a desolation, and no one dwells in them,

3 Because of the evil that they committed, provoking me to anger, in that they went to make offerings and serve other gods that they knew not, neither they, nor you, nor your fathers.

4 Yet I persistently sent to you all my servants the prophets, saying, Oh, do not do this abomination that I hate.

5 But they did not listen or incline their ear, to turn from their evil and make no offerings to other gods.

6 Therefore my wrath and my anger were poured out and kindled in the cities of Yehudah and in the streets of Yerushalayim, and they became a waste and a desolation, as at this day.

7 And now thus says YHWH, the Creator of hosts, the Creator of Yisra'EL: Why do you commit this great evil against yourselves, to cut off from you man and woman, infant and child, from the midst of Yehudah, leaving you no remnant?

8 You provoke me to anger with the works of your hands, making offerings to other gods in the land of Egypt where you have come to live, so that you may be cut off and become a curse and a taunt among all the nations of the earth.

9 Have you forgotten the evil of your fathers, the evil of the kings of Yehudah, the evil of their wives, your own evil, and the evil of your wives, which they committed in the land of Yehudah and in the streets of Yerushalayim?

10 They have not humbled themselves even to this day, nor have they feared, nor walked in my Torah and my statutes that I set before you and before your fathers.

11 Therefore thus says YHWH of hosts, the Creator of Yisra'EL: Behold, I will set my face against you for harm, to cut off all Yehudah.

12 I will take the remnant of Yehudah who have set their faces to come to the land of Egypt to live, and they shall all be consumed. In the land of Egypt they shall fall. By the sword and by famine they shall be consumed, from the least to the greatest. They shall die by the sword and by famine, and they shall become an oath, a horror, a curse, and a taunt.

13 I will punish those who dwell in the land of Egypt, as I have punished Yerushalayim, with the sword, with famine, and with pestilence,

14 So that none of the remnant of Yehudah who have come to live in the land of Egypt shall escape or survive or return to the land of Yehudah, to which they lift up their hearts to return to dwell there. For they shall not return, except some fugitives.

15 Then all the men who knew that their wives had made offerings to other gods, and all the women who stood by, a great assembly, all the people who lived in Patros in the land of Egypt, answered Yirmeyahu:

16 As for the word that you have spoken to us in the name of YHWH, we will not listen to you.

17 But we will do everything that we have vowed, make offerings to the queen of heaven and pour out drink offerings to her, as we did, both we and our fathers, our kings and our officials, in the cities of Yehudah and in the streets of Yerushalayim. For then we had plenty of food, and prospered, and saw no disaster.

18 But since we left off making offerings to the queen of heaven and pouring out drink offerings to her, we have lacked everything and have been consumed by the sword and by famine.

19 And the women said, When we made offerings to the queen of heaven and poured out drink offerings to her, was it without our husbands' approval that we made cakes for her bearing her image and poured out drink offerings to her?

20 Then Yirmeyahu said to all the people, men and women, all the people who had answered him:

21 The offerings that you made in the cities of Yehudah and in the streets of Yerushalayim, you and your fathers, your kings and your officials, and the people of the land — did not YHWH remember them? Did it not come into his mind?

22 YHWH could no longer bear your evil deeds and the abominations that you committed. Therefore your land has become a desolation and a waste and a curse, without inhabitant, as it is this day.

23 It is because you made offerings and because you sinned against YHWH and did not obey the voice of YHWH or walk in his Torah and in his statutes and in his testimonies that this disaster has happened to you, as at this day.

24 Yirmeyahu said to all the people and all the women: Hear the word of YHWH, all you of Yehudah who are in the land of Egypt.

25 Thus says YHWH of hosts, the Creator of Yisra'EL: You and your wives have declared with your mouths, and have fulfilled it with your hands, saying, We will surely perform our vows that we have made, to make offerings to the queen of heaven and to pour out drink offerings to her. Then confirm your vows and perform your vows.

26 Therefore hear the word of YHWH, all you of Yehudah who dwell in the land of Egypt: Behold, I have sworn by my great name, says YHWH, that my name shall no more be invoked by the mouth of any man of Yehudah in all the land of Egypt, saying, As the Creator YHWH lives.

27 Behold, I am watching over them for harm and not for good, and all the men of Yehudah who are in the land of Egypt shall be consumed by the sword and by famine, until there is an end of them.

28 And those who escape the sword shall return from the land of Egypt to the land of Yehudah, few in number. And all the remnant of Yehudah, who came to the land of Egypt to live, shall know whose word will stand, mine or theirs.

29 This shall be the sign to you, declares YHWH, that I will punish you in this place, in order that you may know that my words will surely stand against you for harm:

30 Thus says YHWH, Behold, I will give Pharaoh Khofra king of Egypt into the hand of his enemies and into the hand of those who seek his life, as I gave Tzidkiyahu king of Yehudah into the hand of Nevukhadnetzar king of Babylon, who was his enemy and sought his life.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The DSS shorter recension preserves Chapter 44 in close agreement with the Masoretic. Chapter 44 is Yirmeyahu's final recorded prophetic confrontation — in Egypt, with the remnant who fled to Egypt. The covenant rebellion is total: the people explicitly reject the covenant word — verse 16 — and attribute their prosperity to the queen of heaven rather than to YHWH.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 44 in full. Verse 16 — we will not listen to you — is the most direct covenant rejection in the entire book. After forty years of prophetic ministry, the covenant people say explicitly what they have been saying implicitly throughout: we will not obey the voice of YHWH through you.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 44. Verse 17-18 — the people's covenant inversion: when we offered to the queen of heaven we prospered — when we stopped we suffered. The covenant memory is inverted: they attribute Yehudah's prosperity to queen-of-heaven worship and its destruction to abandoning that worship. The covenant reality is the exact opposite.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 28 — all the remnant shall know whose word will stand, mine or theirs. The covenant ultimatum reduced to its starkest form: the covenant contest is between YHWH's covenant word and the people's covenant rebellion. Time will decide the covenant victor. The few who return will be the living covenant proof. The majority who perish in Egypt will be the covenant evidence. The covenant word always stands — the only question is whether the covenant community will be on the right side of it when it does.

YIRMEYAHU — CHAPTER 45 The Word to Barukh — You Said Woe Is Me — I Will Bring Disaster — Your Life as a Prize of War

1 The word that Yirmeyahu the prophet spoke to Barukh the son of Neriyahu, when he wrote these words in a book at the dictation of Yirmeyahu, in the fourth year of Yeho'yakim the son of Yoshiyahu, king of Yehudah:

2 Thus says YHWH, the Creator of Yisra'EL, to you, O Barukh:

3 You said, Woe is me, for YHWH has added sorrow to my pain. I am weary with my groaning, and I find no rest.

4 Thus shall you say to him: Thus says YHWH — Behold, what I have built I am breaking down, and what I have planted I am plucking up — that is, the whole land.

5 And do you seek great things for yourself? Seek them not, for behold, I am bringing disaster upon all flesh, declares YHWH. But I will give you your life as a prize of war in all places to which you may go.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The DSS shorter recension preserves Chapter 45 in close agreement with the Masoretic. Chapter 45 is the covenant word to Barukh — the prophet's faithful scribe who wrote the covenant words. The placement of this chapter — dated to the fourth year of Yeho'yakim, before the events of Chapters 36-44 — is the covenant editorial placement that addresses Barukh's personal covenant crisis before the reader encounters the destruction he witnessed.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 45 in full. Verse 5 — do you seek great things for yourself? Seek them not — I will give you your life as a prize of war. The covenant promise to Barukh is identical to the covenant promise to Eved-Melekh in Chapter 39:18: life as a prize of war. Covenant fidelity to the covenant prophet receives covenant protection, not covenant prosperity.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 45. Verse 4 — what I have built I am breaking down, and what I have planted I am plucking up — that is, the whole land. The covenant word to Barukh places his personal sorrow within the cosmic covenant framework: YHWH is tearing down the whole covenant structure. Barukh's personal grief is real but must be measured against the scale of what YHWH himself is doing.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 5 — do you seek great things for yourself? Seek them not. The covenant rebuke of personal ambition in the context of covenant catastrophe. Barukh has faithfully served the covenant prophet — written the covenant words, read them publicly, preserved them after the scroll was burned. His reward is life, not greatness. The covenant standard for the covenant community's servants in times of judgment is survival, not success. Covenant faithfulness in catastrophic times is measured by perseverance, not achievement.

ORACLES AGAINST THE NATIONS — CHAPTERS 46 THROUGH 48

YIRMEYAHU — CHAPTER 46 The Oracle Against Egypt — The Battle of Karkemish — Egypt Is Like the Nile — I Will Punish Amon of No — Fear Not O Yaakov

1 The word of YHWH that came to Yirmeyahu the prophet concerning the nations.

2 Concerning Egypt: about the army of Pharaoh Nekho, king of Egypt, which was by the Euphrates River at Karkemish and which Nevukhadnetzar king of Babylon defeated in the fourth year of Yeho'yakim the son of Yoshiyahu, king of Yehudah:

3 Prepare buckler and shield, and advance for battle.

4 Harness the horses — mount, O horsemen. Take your stations with your helmets, polish your spears, put on your armor.

5 Why have I seen it? They are dismayed and have turned backward. Their warriors are beaten down and have fled in haste. They look not back — terror on every side, declares YHWH.

6 The swift cannot flee away, nor the warrior escape. In the north by the river Euphrates they have stumbled and fallen.

7 Who is this, rising like the Nile, like rivers whose waters surge?

8 Egypt rises like the Nile, like rivers whose waters surge. He said, I will rise — I will cover the earth — I will destroy cities and their inhabitants.

9 Advance, O horses, and rage, O chariots. Let the warriors go out: men of Kush and Put who handle the shield, men of Lud, skilled in handling and bending the bow.

10 That day is the day of YHWH of hosts, a day of vengeance, to avenge himself on his foes. The sword shall devour and be sated and drink its fill of their blood. For YHWH of hosts holds a sacrifice in the north country by the river Euphrates.

11 Go up to Gil'ad, and take balm, O virgin daughter of Egypt. In vain you have used many medicines — there is no healing for you.

12 The nations have heard of your shame, and the earth is full of your cry. For warrior has stumbled against warrior — they have both fallen together.

13 The word that YHWH spoke to Yirmeyahu the prophet about the coming of Nevukhadnetzar king of Babylon to strike the land of Egypt:

14 Declare in Egypt, and proclaim in Migdol — proclaim in Nof and Takhpanes. Say: Stand ready and be prepared, for the sword shall devour around you.

15 Why are your mighty ones face down? They do not stand because YHWH thrust them down.

16 He made many stumble, and they fell, and they said one to another: Arise, and let us go back to our own people and to the land of our birth, because of the sword of the oppressor.

17 Call the name of Pharaoh, king of Egypt: Noisy one who lets the hour go by.

18 As I live, declares the King, whose name is YHWH of hosts, like Tabor among the mountains and like Karmel by the sea, shall one come.

19 Prepare yourselves baggage for exile, O inhabitants of Egypt. For Nof shall become a waste and desolate, without inhabitant.

20 Egypt is a beautiful heifer, but a biting fly from the north has come upon her.

21 Even her hired soldiers in her midst are like fattened calves. They too have turned and fled together — they did not stand, for the day of their calamity has come upon them, the time of their punishment.

22 Her sound is like a fleeing serpent, for her enemies march in force and come against her with axes, like those who fell trees.

23 They shall cut down her forest, declares YHWH, though it is impenetrable, because they are more numerous than locusts — they are without number.

24 The daughter of Egypt shall be put to shame. She shall be delivered into the hand of a people from the north.

25 YHWH of hosts, the Creator of Yisra'EL, said: Behold, I am bringing punishment upon Amon of No, and Pharaoh and Egypt and her gods and her kings, upon Pharaoh and those who trust in him.

26 I will deliver them into the hand of those who seek their lives, into the hand of Nevukhadnetzar king of Babylon and his officers. Afterward Egypt shall be inhabited as in the days of old, declares YHWH.

27 But fear not, O Yaakov my servant, nor be dismayed, O Yisra'EL. For behold, I will save you from far away, and your offspring from the land of their captivity. Yaakov shall return and have quiet and ease, and none shall make him afraid.

28 Fear not, O Yaakov my servant, declares YHWH, for I am with you. I will make a full end of all the nations to which I have driven you, but of you I will not make a full end. I will discipline you in just measure, and I will by no means leave you unpunished.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The DSS shorter recension of the oracles against the nations differs significantly in arrangement from the Masoretic. In the DSS and Septuagint, the oracles against the nations are placed at the center of the book — after Chapter 25. The Masoretic places them at the end — Chapters 46-51. The DSS is the primary text; the arrangement here follows the Masoretic placement for the English scholarly edition while noting the DSS structural difference.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 46 in full. Verse 9 — men of Kush and Put who handle the shield, men of Lud, skilled in handling and bending the bow — is the covenant catalog of Egyptian allied forces that includes the northeast African corridor peoples. The Kushite soldiers of Egypt are named within the covenant oracle against Egypt.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic preserves the oracles against the nations in Chapters 46-51. Verse 25 — I am bringing punishment upon Amon of No — the Theban deity Amun, whose center of worship was No — modern Luxor — is named as the covenant target of YHWH's judgment. YHWH's covenant judgment reaches into the heart of the Egyptian theological system.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 27-28 — fear not, O Yaakov my servant — the covenant consolation embedded within the oracle against Egypt is addressed to Yisra'EL, not Egypt. The same formula as Chapter 30:10-11. The covenant pattern: even in the oracles against the nations, YHWH's covenant concern for Yaakov is present. The nations' judgment is not the end — covenant Yaakov's restoration is the covenant purpose behind the nations' judgment.

YIRMEYAHU — CHAPTER 47 The Oracle Against the Pelishtim — Waters Rising From the North — The Sword of YHWH — Aza Has Been Shaved Bare

1 The word of YHWH that came to Yirmeyahu the prophet concerning the Pelishtim, before Pharaoh struck down Aza.

2 Thus says YHWH: Behold, waters are rising out of the north, and shall become an overflowing torrent. They shall overflow the land and all that fills it, the city and those who dwell in it. People shall cry out, and every inhabitant of the land shall wail.

3 At the noise of the stamping of the hoofs of his stallions, at the rushing of his chariots, at the rumbling of their wheels, the fathers look not back to their children, so feeble are their hands,

4 Because of the day that is coming to destroy all the Pelishtim, to cut off from Tzor and Tzidon every helper that remains. For YHWH is destroying the Pelishtim, the remnant of the coastland of Kaftor.

5 Baldness has come upon Aza — Ashkelon has perished. O remnant of their valley, how long will you gash yourselves?

6 Ah, sword of YHWH, how long till you are quiet? Put yourself into your scabbard — rest and be still.

7 How can it be quiet when YHWH has given it a charge? Against Ashkelon and against the seashore he has appointed it.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The DSS shorter recension preserves Chapter 47 in close agreement with the Masoretic. The oracle against the Pelishtim is the covenant judgment against the people of the coastal plain — Kaftor, the Aegean-origin identity of the Pelishtim — who have been in covenant conflict with Yisra'EL from the period of the Shofetim. The waters from the north — Babylon — will overwhelm even the coastal strongholds.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 47 in full. Verse 6-7 — ah, sword of YHWH, how long till you are quiet? How can it be quiet when YHWH has given it a charge? The covenant sword has a covenant commission that cannot be recalled by human petition. The sword of YHWH is quiet only when the covenant appointment is fulfilled.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 47. Verse 4 — YHWH is destroying the Pelishtim, the remnant of the coastland of Kaftor. The covenant identification of the Pelishtim as coastal people of Kaphtorite origin is the geographic and genealogical context of the covenant judgment: they came from the sea, and from the north they will be destroyed.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 6 — ah, sword of YHWH, how long till you are quiet? The covenant lament within the covenant oracle: even as YHWH's judgment is pronounced, the text acknowledges its devastating totality. The sword of YHWH that devours through Babylon cannot be stayed by sympathy or by covenant petition. The covenant commission given to the sword determines its course and its conclusion.

YIRMEYAHU — CHAPTER 48 The Oracle Against Moav — Moav Is Destroyed — Moav Has Been at Ease From His Youth — The Captivity of Moav

1 Concerning Moav. Thus says YHWH of hosts, the Creator of Yisra'EL: Woe to Nebo, for it is laid waste. Kiriyatayim is put to shame, it is taken. The fortress is put to shame and broken down.

2 The renown of Moav is no more. In Heshbon they planned disaster against her: Come, let us cut her off from being a nation. You also, O Madmen, shall be brought to silence. The sword shall pursue you.

3 A voice — a cry from Horonayim, desolation and great destruction.

4 Moav is destroyed. Her little ones have made a cry.

5 For at the ascent of Luhit they go up weeping. For at the descent of Horonayim they have heard the distressed cry of destruction.

6 Flee. Save yourselves. You shall be like a juniper in the desert.

7 For because you trusted in your works and your treasures, you also shall be taken. And Kemosh shall go into exile with his priests and his officials.

8 The destroyer shall come upon every city, and no city shall escape. The valley shall perish, and the plain shall be destroyed, as YHWH has spoken.

9 Give wings to Moav, for she would fly away. Her cities shall become a desolation, with no inhabitant in them.

10 Cursed is he who does the work of YHWH with slackness, and cursed is he who keeps back his sword from bloodshed.

11 Moav has been at ease from his youth and has settled on his dregs. He has not been emptied from vessel to vessel, nor has he gone into exile. So his taste remains in him, and his scent is not changed.

12 Therefore, behold, the days are coming, declares YHWH, when I shall send to him pourers who will pour him, and empty his vessels and break his jars in pieces.

13 Then Moav shall be ashamed of Kemosh, as the house of Yisra'EL was ashamed of Beit-El, their confidence.

14 How do you say, We are heroes and mighty men of war?

15 The destroyer of Moav and his cities has come up, and the choicest of his young men have gone down to slaughter, declares the King, whose name is YHWH of hosts.

16 The calamity of Moav is near at hand, and his affliction hastens swiftly.

17 Grieve for him, all you who are around him, and all who know his name. Say, How the mighty scepter is broken, the glorious staff.

18 Come down from your glory, and sit on the parched ground, O inhabitant of Dibon. For the destroyer of Moav has come up against you. He has destroyed your strongholds.

19 Stand by the way and watch, O inhabitant of Aro'er. Ask him who flees and her who escapes. Say: What has happened?

20 Moav is put to shame, for it is broken. Wail and cry. Tell it by the Arnon, that Moav is laid waste.

21 Judgment has come upon the tableland, upon Holon, and Yahtzah, and Meifaat,

22 And Divon, and Nebo, and Beit-Divlatayim,

23 And Kiryatayim, and Beit-Gamul, and Beit-Me'on,

24 And Keriyot, and Botzrah, and all the cities of the land of Moav, far and near.

25 The horn of Moav is cut off, and his arm is broken, declares YHWH.

26 Make him drunk, because he magnified himself against YHWH, so that Moav shall wallow in his vomit, and he too shall be held in derision.

27 Was not Yisra'EL a derision to you? Was he found among thieves, that whenever you spoke of him you wagged your head?

28 Leave the cities and dwell in the rock, O inhabitants of Moav. Be like the dove that nests in the sides of the mouth of a gorge.

29 We have heard of the pride of Moav — he is very proud — of his loftiness, his pride, and his arrogance, and the haughtiness of his heart.

30 I know his insolence, declares YHWH. His boasts are false — his deeds are false.

31 Therefore I wail for Moav. I cry out for all Moav. For the men of Kir-Heres I mourn.

32 More than for Yazer I weep for you, O vine of Sivmah. Your branches passed over the sea — they reached to the sea of Yazer. On your summer fruits and your grape harvest the destroyer has fallen.

33 Gladness and joy have been taken away from the fruitful land of Moav. I have made the wine cease from the winepresses — no one treads them with shouts of joy. The shouting is not the shout of joy.

34 From the cry of Heshbon to El'aleh, as far as Yahatz they utter their voice, from Tzo'ar to Horonayim and Eglat-Shelishiyah. For the waters of Nimrim also have become desolate.

35 And I will bring to an end in Moav, declares YHWH, him who offers sacrifice at the high place and makes offerings to his god.

36 Therefore my heart moans for Moav like flutes, and my heart moans like flutes for the men of Kir-Heres. Therefore the riches they gained have perished.

37 For every head is shaved and every beard cut off. On all the hands are gashes, and around the waist is sackcloth.

38 On all the housetops of Moav and in the squares there is nothing but lamentation, for I have broken Moav like a vessel for which no one cares, declares YHWH.

39 How it is broken. How they wail. How Moav has turned his back in shame. So Moav has become a derision and a horror to all that are around him.

40 For thus says YHWH: Behold, one shall fly swiftly like an eagle and spread his wings against Moav.

41 The cities shall be taken and the strongholds seized. The heart of the warriors of Moav shall be in that day like the heart of a woman in her birth pains.

42 Moav shall be destroyed and be no longer a people, because he magnified himself against YHWH.

43 Terror, pit, and snare are before you, O inhabitant of Moav, declares YHWH.

44 He who flees from the terror shall fall into the pit, and he who climbs out of the pit shall be caught in the snare. For I will bring these things upon Moav, the year of their punishment, declares YHWH.

45 In the shadow of Heshbon fugitives stop without strength, for fire came out from Heshbon, flame from the house of Sikhon. It has destroyed the forehead of Moav and the crown of the head of the sons of tumult.

46 Woe to you, O Moav. The people of Kemosh are undone, for your sons have been taken captive and your daughters into captivity.

47 Yet I will restore the fortunes of Moav in the latter days, declares YHWH. Thus far is the judgment on Moav.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The DSS shorter recension of Chapter 48 differs from the Masoretic in several verses. The oracle against Moav is the longest of the oracles against the nations. The covenant relationship between Yisra'EL and Moav runs from Bereshit through Ruth — Moav is the covenant border nation. The covenant judgment on Moav is proportional to Moav's history of covenant hostility toward Yisra'EL.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 48 in full. Verse 47 — yet I will restore the fortunes of Moav in the latter days — is the covenant universalism that closes even the most severe covenant judgment oracle: YHWH's covenant purpose ultimately includes even Moav. The same restoration formula applied to Yisra'EL applies at the end of the Moav oracle.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic preserves the longest version of the Moav oracle. Verse 11 — Moav has been at ease from his youth and has settled on his dregs — he has not been emptied from vessel to vessel. The covenant image of wine that has never been decanted: Moav's undisturbed national existence has allowed the sediment of pride and complacency to settle permanently. The disaster is the violent decanting that breaks the vessel.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 36 — my heart moans for Moav like flutes. Even in the covenant oracle of judgment, YHWH's covenant grief for the covenant condemned is present. The same covenant sorrow that Yirmeyahu expresses for Yisra'EL — Chapter 8:21, Chapter 9:1 — YHWH expresses for Moav. The covenant Creator grieves the covenant destruction of every people, not only of the covenant people. Covenant judgment and covenant sorrow co-exist in YHWH's covenant character.

END OF PART SIX — CHAPTERS 40 THROUGH 48

Volume Eighteen — Yirmeyahu — Part Six

𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄

CONCLUDES IN PART SEVEN — CHAPTERS 49 THROUGH 52
